food_name: Bánh Mì Thịt Nướng (Baguette with Grilled Pork)
aliases: Baguette With Grilled Pork/Bánh Mì Thịt Nướng

english: Baguette With Grilled Pork/Bánh Mì Thịt Nướng

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for bánh mì. 'Sandwich food product' (FOODON_03315763) is the closest defensible parent class for a filled baguette-style bread product.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Sandwich) is the closest defensible match for a filled baguette. No specific bánh mì code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for bánh mì or Vietnamese filled baguette as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bánh mì sandwich (approximately 200 g) consisting of a Vietnamese-style baguette filled with grilled marinated pork, pickled vegetables, pâté, mayonnaise, cucumber, coriander, and chili.

nutrient_source.title: Baguette With Grilled Pork / Bánh Mì Thịt Nướng — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Baguette With Grilled Pork / Bánh Mì Thịt Nướng (cr_id G00010551). Carbohydrate is recorded as 0.0 g/100g in the HPB source; this is a known data anomaly for this composite dish — see notes. Saturated fat is not reported in the HPB entry and is estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork and baguette component data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bánh mì thịt nướng as a composite dish. GI of 72 is assigned based on the dominant carbohydrate component: French baguette (white wheat flour), for which the University of Sydney GI Database reports GI = 72 (±5) using glucose reference. The recorded carbohydrate value of 0.0 g/100g in the HPB source is a known data anomaly; the actual carbohydrate contribution of the baguette component is approximately 45–55 g/100g of bread. Glycemic load per serving is calculated as approximately 0 using the anomalous HPB carbohydrate figure; a corrected GL estimate based on realistic carbohydrate content (~25 g available carbohydrate per 200 g serving) would be approximately 18, which is high. The GI category is classified as 'high' based on the baguette component.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold; fish sauce, soy sauce, pickled vegetables, pâté, and mayonnaise are the primary contributors
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit bánh mì thịt nướng due to its extreme sodium load of 1050 mg/100g; if consumed, request reduced fish sauce and soy sauce in the marinade, minimal pickled vegetables, and omission of pâté and mayonnaise.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined white flour baguette (GI ~72) with pork marinade containing added sugar; the HPB-recorded carbohydrate of 0 g/100g is a data anomaly — actual carbohydrate from the baguette component is 45–55 g/100g of bread
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size of bánh mì thịt nướng due to the high-GI refined white flour baguette and sugar-containing pork marinade; request a wholegrain bread substitute where available and pair with non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1050 mg/100g), moderate saturated fat from pork and pâté (~3.2 g/100g estimated), and cholesterol at 89 mg/100g collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit bánh mì thịt nướng due to its high sodium content, saturated fat from pork and pâté, and cholesterol load; request reduced condiments, omit pâté, and choose leaner pork cuts to reduce cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium worsens tissue swelling; pork and pâté contribute arachidonic acid (omega-6); refined white flour drives high glycemic load promoting inflammatory mediators; chili (nightshade) may trigger individual RA flares
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit bánh mì thịt nướng due to its high sodium, pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid from pork and pâté, high-GI baguette, and nightshade chili content; reduce fish sauce, soy sauce, and pickled vegetables, and consider omitting pâté and chili.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh Mì Thịt Nướng (Baguette with Grilled Pork) is a Vietnamese-origin filled baguette sandwich that has become a popular street food and café item in Singapore. The dish consists of a light, crispy French-style baguette made from refined white wheat flour, split and filled with grilled marinated pork (thịt nướng), which is typically marinated in a mixture of fish sauce, soy sauce, lemongrass, garlic, sugar, and five-spice powder before being grilled over charcoal or a gas flame. The sandwich is assembled with pickled shredded carrots and daikon radish (đồ chua), sliced cucumber, fresh coriander (cilantro), sliced chili, pork or chicken liver pâté, and mayonnaise. The flavour profile is complex: savoury, smoky, tangy from the pickles, herbaceous from the coriander, and mildly spicy from the chili. The texture contrasts a crisp, airy baguette crust with tender, juicy grilled pork and crunchy pickled vegetables. Nutritionally, the HPB Singapore database records 171 kcal/100g, 19.1 g protein, 10.5 g fat, and 1050 mg sodium per 100g. The carbohydrate value of 0.0 g/100g in the HPB record is a known data anomaly for this composite dish; the baguette component realistically contributes 45–55 g carbohydrate per 100g of bread. Clinically, the dish is high concern for hypertension (sodium 1050 mg/100g), type 2 diabetes (high-GI baguette, sugar in marinade), and cardiovascular disease (sodium, saturated fat, cholesterol). The dish is not halal due to pork content. A standard serving is approximately 200 g.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010551, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork and baguette component data. The carbohydrate anomaly (0.0 g/100g) is documented and does not reduce confidence in the HPB-sourced values; it reflects a known data gap in the composite dish record.
notes: CARBOHYDRATE ANOMALY: carbohydrate_g is recorded as 0.0 g/100g in the HPB Singapore source for this composite dish. This is a known data anomaly — a standard Vietnamese baguette contributes approximately 45–55 g carbohydrate per 100g of bread, and the composite dish would realistically contain significant carbohydrate. The value is preserved as reported by HPB (confirmed zero per HPB source) but flagged for review. Sugar_g is similarly 0.0 g/100g per HPB — confirmed zero per HPB source, though the pork marinade contains added sugar; this likely reflects the composite dish averaging. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 g/100g per HPB — confirmed zero per HPB source; the baguette component would realistically contribute <2 g/100g fibre. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION: carbohydrate_g, sugar_g, and dietary_fibre_g are all reported as 0.0 g/100g — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010551); however, these values are flagged as likely data anomalies for this composite dish and should be reviewed against a full recipe-level nutrient calculation. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this composite dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (cr_id G00010551) and are not available from a single approved source for this exact preparation as of 2026. SATURATED FAT: saturated_fat_g of 3.2 g/100g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for grilled pork loin and French baguette components (USDA FDC IDs 168351 and 174924 respectively), weighted by approximate ingredient proportions; this is an adapted estimate. GI NOTE: GI of 72 is assigned based on the French baguette component (University of Sydney GI Database); the HPB carbohydrate anomaly means glycemic load per serving is calculated as 0.0 using the HPB figure, but a corrected estimate based on realistic carbohydrate content would yield GL ~18 (high). HALAL STATUS: this dish contains pork and is not halal.
